authors_affiliation: Philipp Sodmann, Marcus Vollmer; Institute of Bioinformatics, University Medicine Greifswald / DZHK (German Centre for Cardiovascular Research), Greifswald, Germany

result.evaluation_split: official hidden test set (28th of 41 teams); also report a 5-fold cross-validation challenge metric of 0.113 on the training data

self_reported_metrics.other: On a validation split, only 8 of 24 scored classes reached F1 > 0.5; best per-class F1: atrial fibrillation 0.87, sinus rhythm 0.82, left bundle branch block 0.77. Segmentation sub-network achieved F1 up to 0.98 (QRS-complex, 20ms tolerance), 0.99 (P-wave, 20ms tolerance) and 0.88 (T-wave, 20ms tolerance) on a held-out annotated test split; R-peak F1 of 0.943 at 150ms tolerance on MIT-BIH.

model.architecture_details: Two-stage pipeline: (1) a UNet-like fully convolutional 1D network (4 down/upsampling levels with skip connections, 50% dropout in bottleneck layers, batchnorm + Mish activation) accepts variable-length 12-lead input and segments the ECG into P-wave/QRS-complex/T-wave regions while also classifying each QRS complex as normal, supraventricular, or ventricular extrasystole; (2) 54 hand-crafted features (timing intervals, wave amplitudes, Frank-lead vectorcardiographic angles, extrasystole proportions) derived from the segmentation output are classified with an XGBoost gradient-boosted-tree model (24 non-exclusive labels).
model.key_novelty: Uses a UNet segmentation network to produce human-interpretable intermediate ECG delineations (P/QRS/T boundaries and beat classification) and derives classical clinical features from these segments for downstream tree-based classification, prioritizing explainability over an end-to-end black-box classifier.

input.signal_length_window: Arbitrary length (network input shape (None, 12) accepts ECGs of any duration); manually annotated training/validation/test snippets were up to 20s
input.preprocessing: Resampled to 500Hz via FFT; bandpass filtered 0.05-42Hz; discrete wavelet transform (Daubechies Db4) with hard thresholding applied as a high-pass filter to remove residual baseline wander

data.training_datasets: Official Challenge training data (all sources) for the XGBoost classifier; the UNet segmentation network was trained on a custom-built annotated corpus (573 train / 94 validation / 73 test ECGs, built via an in-house annotation tool, one physician annotator); a separate 740-ECG annotated set was used in a preliminary auxiliary-output experiment not included in the final submission

training.loss_function: Tversky loss (segmentation output); categorical cross-entropy (extrasystole classification output)
training.optimizer: Rectified Adam with LookAhead (Ranger); cosine-annealing learning-rate decay to zero after epoch 140; trained for 200 epochs total
training.cross_validation_strategy: 5-fold cross-validation (via hyperopt tree-structured Parzen estimators) used to tune XGBoost hyperparameters; UNet trained/validated/tested on fixed manually annotated splits (573/94/73 ECGs)

contribution.summary: Proposes a two-step, explainable pipeline that first segments 12-lead ECGs into P/QRS/T waves and beat types with a UNet, then extracts classical clinical features from the segmentation for XGBoost classification, trading raw classification performance for interpretability.
